text: FGSM (Goodfellow et al. 2015) generates adversarial examples by adding ε·sign(∇_x L(x,y)) to input, demonstrating that imperceptible perturbations can cause high-confidence misclassification.

PGD (Madry et al. 2018, MIT) is the strongest first-order adversarial attack — iteratively applying FGSM with projection — and adversarial training with PGD became the standard defense for certified robustness.

Adversarial training, where models are trained on adversarially perturbed examples, is the most effective empirical defense, but certified defenses using interval bound propagation provide provable guarantees.
